questions.question: Which country held a presidential election in January 2024 that resulted in a pro-independence candidate winning?
questions.explanation: Lai Ching-te of the DPP won Taiwan's presidential election in January 2024.

questions.question: Which social media platform was temporarily banned in the US in January 2024 before being restored?
questions.explanation: While TikTok faced legislative threats in 2024, no major platform was fully banned in Q1.

questions.question: Who won the Super Bowl LVIII in February 2024?
questions.explanation: The Kansas City Chiefs defeated the 49ers 25-22 in overtime.

questions.question: Which country became the 4th to land a spacecraft on the Moon in January 2024?
questions.explanation: Japan's SLIM lander successfully touched down on the Moon on January 19, 2024.

questions.question: Which AI company's chatbot went viral for generating realistic video from text prompts in February 2024?
questions.explanation: OpenAI's Sora AI model generated impressive videos from text prompts.

questions.question: Who won the Republican primary in New Hampshire in January 2024?
questions.explanation: Donald Trump won the New Hampshire Republican primary.

questions.question: Which European country's farmers staged massive protests blocking highways in early 2024?
questions.explanation: French farmers blocked roads protesting EU regulations and low prices in January-February 2024.

questions.question: What was the main reason for the collapse of the Francis Scott Key Bridge in Baltimore?
questions.explanation: The container ship Dali struck the bridge on March 26, 2024, causing its collapse.

questions.question: Which monarch was diagnosed with cancer in February 2024?
questions.explanation: King Charles III announced his cancer diagnosis on February 5, 2024.

questions.question: Kate Middleton revealed what personal news in March 2024?
options: She was stepping down from duties
questions.explanation: The Princess of Wales announced she was undergoing cancer treatment in March 2024.

questions.question: Which tech company became the first to reach a $3 trillion market cap in 2024?
questions.explanation: Microsoft briefly surpassed Apple to become the most valuable company in early 2024.

questions.question: Which country held a general election in February 2024 where the opposition party won?
questions.explanation: Indonesia held elections in February 2024, with Prabowo Subianto winning the presidency.

questions.question: The Oscars 2024 Best Picture went to which film?
questions.explanation: Oppenheimer won Best Picture at the 96th Academy Awards in March 2024.

questions.question: Which country faced a major earthquake in January 2024 killing over 200 people?
questions.explanation: A 7.6 magnitude earthquake struck Japan's Noto Peninsula on January 1, 2024.

questions.question: Which tech company laid off approximately 12,000 employees in January 2024?
questions.explanation: Google laid off hundreds of employees across multiple divisions in January 2024.
